name: James Burgess
subtitle: Marine Conservation Volunteer
title: Environmental Advocate & Reef Monitoring Specialist
valueProp: I combine diving expertise with data collection skills to support critical marine conservation efforts. Passionate about protecting ocean ecosystems through hands-on fieldwork, community education, and evidence-based advocacy.
about: I'm a dedicated marine conservation volunteer with over 300 logged conservation dives and 3 years of active fieldwork experience. My work spans reef health monitoring, marine species surveys, and ocean cleanup initiatives across South Africa's coastline.

I hold PADI certifications including Dive Master and multiple conservation specialties (AWARE Coral Reef Conservation, Shark Conservation, Marine Debris). I bring both diving proficiency and genuine scientific curiosity to every project.

My technical background in data engineering translates directly to conservation work—I excel at collecting, organizing, and analyzing field data for research teams. I've contributed observations to Wildbook and other marine conservation databases.

Beyond fieldwork, I'm passionate about education and advocacy. I believe lasting conservation requires community engagement, and I volunteer time teaching responsible diving practices and leading beach cleanup initiatives.

mainCert.title: PADI Dive Master + AWARE Conservation Specialist
mainCert.experience: Reef Surveys, Species ID, Data Collection, Underwater Photography, Debris Removal

additionalCerts: AWARE Coral Reef Conservation • AWARE Shark Conservation • Project AWARE Specialist • Marine Debris Specialist

experience.title: Marine Conservation Volunteer
experience.subtitle: Reef Monitoring & Species Conservation
points: Conducted 50+ coral reef health assessments using standardized monitoring protocols
Contributed to fish population surveys documenting species diversity and abundance
Collected photographic evidence for marine species identification databases
Participated in long-term reef monitoring programs tracking ecosystem changes
Logged observations in Wildbook and other scientific conservation platforms

experience.title: Ocean Cleanup Coordinator
experience.subtitle: Marine Debris Removal & Community Engagement
points: Organized and led 30+ underwater cleanup dives removing ghost nets and debris
Coordinated community beach cleanup events with 100+ total participants
Catalogued and reported marine debris findings to conservation databases
Educated divers and beachgoers on plastic pollution and responsible practices
Removed over 500kg of marine debris from local reef and beach sites

experience.title: Conservation Educator
experience.subtitle: Marine Awareness & Responsible Diving Education
points: Developed and delivered marine conservation presentations for local schools
Taught responsible diving practices to recreational dive groups
Created educational content on social media promoting ocean conservation
Mentored new conservation volunteers on survey techniques and protocols
Advocated for marine protected areas through community engagement

commitment.title: Let's Protect Our Oceans
commitment.subtitle: Available for volunteer projects or conservation roles
commitment.text: I'm seeking opportunities to contribute to meaningful marine conservation projects—whether that's reef monitoring, species surveys, research support, or community education. I bring diving skills, data competency, and genuine dedication to protecting ocean ecosystems for future generations.
